Overall:
The following processes have been demonstrated to be effective for the removal of propoxur from drinking water: ozone (>99 percent removal) and UV in the presence of a photocatalyst such as titanium dioxide, 2,4,6-triphenylpyrylium-Zeolite, zeolite supported TiO2 and GAC supported TiO2 (up to 100 degradation can be achieved). Performance of these technologies can vary based on water quality and treatment characteristics.
Chlorine and chlorine dioxide were demonstrated to provide no significant removal of propoxur.
